steps.title: Identify the boundary between two independent clauses
steps.content: The blank sits between two independent clauses. The first is “A ray diagram reveals how this works.” The second is “Because the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray, all light passing through the hole can only arrive at a single destination, eliminating diffraction and ensuring a clear image.” These must be properly separated or connected.

steps.title: Apply the correct sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
The correct answer uses a period to create a clear sentence break, then starts a new sentence with a dependent clause (Because the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray) followed by a comma and an independent clause. A period marks a sentence boundary, and a dependent clause followed by a comma and an independent clause is standard, correct structure.

The other options fail to properly separate the clauses:
  • A leaves them ambiguously connected in one sentence
  • C creates a comma splice
  • D ambiguously connects them after “because”

That is the correct answer.
